title: Lipstick on Silk
timeEstimate: Pro-lane within hours; cold blot only at home
stainChemistry: Lipstick is four-part: wax (beeswax / carnauba) + oil (castor / lanolin) + pigment (iron oxides + lakes) + dye (D&C reds). The wax forms a hydrophobic shell around pigment + dye, requiring solvent to penetrate. On silk, the cotton lipstick-removal toolkit fails: rubbing alcohol strips silk dyes, acetone destroys silk, peroxide yellows silk. Pro uses cold POG / petroleum ether for the wax + oil layer + cold NSD for residual pigment + sodium hydrosulfite for residual dye color. Home cannot match this safely.
whyThisWorks: Cold solvent penetrates the wax shell to release pigment + dye, then NSD lifts the dissolved components. Silk-safe reducing bleach for residual dye color. Home protocol = blot wet bulk, no chemistry, pro within hours.
defaultAssumption: Silk + lipstick = pro within hours. Home blots wet bulk, then escalates. Long-wear / matte lipsticks have more pigment + polymer = harder. Sheer / gloss lipsticks = slightly easier.

spottingProtocol.instruction: Use the FLAT edge of a plastic card to lift the bulk waxy deposit off. Don't scrape or smear. Lift, place on cloth, wipe card.
Cold solvent penetrates the wax shell. Tight blotting from edge inward, refresh cloth each pass.
Cold NSD lifts dissolved pigment. Press only, no agitation.
For residual D&C red dye color, reducing bleach is silk-safer than oxidizer. Strict 60-sec dwell.
Final cold rinse. Press between dry towels, lay flat to air-dry.

steps: 1. Plastic card flat-edge lift of waxy bulk.
2. Cold POG / petroleum ether for wax + oil.
3. Cold NSD sponge press for pigment.
4. Sodium hydrosulfite for residual dye, 60-sec ceiling.
5. Cold final rinse, press dry flat.
warnings: NEVER isopropyl alcohol on silk — strips silk dyes
NEVER acetone on silk — destroys fibroin
NEVER hydrogen peroxide on silk — yellows dyes

homeSolutions.instruction: Lipstick on silk is the rare combination where the cotton toolkit damages silk. Rubbing alcohol (the standard home lipstick remover) STRIPS SILK DYES. Acetone destroys silk fibroin. Peroxide yellows silk. Even the gentlest home detergent can drive lipstick deeper if applied with friction. Home protocol = lift bulk wax, cold-water back-flush, then pro within hours.

homeSolutions.agent: Lift Bulk Wax — Plastic Card Flat Edge
homeSolutions.instruction: Use the FLAT edge of a plastic card (gift card, credit card) to lift the waxy bulk off. Don't scrape or smear — lift the deposit straight up off the silk. Wipe the card on a separate cloth between passes. Most surface waxy bulk comes off cleanly without touching silk.

homeSolutions.agent: Cold Water Back-Flush — Pour Through, Not On Top
homeSolutions.instruction: Turn inside out. Place a white towel under the stain. Run COLD water through the BACK of the stain into the catch towel. Cold ONLY. Pour through the silk, never pooled on top — water rings silk. 60 seconds usually pulls some pigment to a pale shadow.

homeSolutions.agent: Press Dry — DO NOT Apply Anything Else
homeSolutions.instruction: Press silk between two dry white towels. No wringing. Lay flat at room temperature, out of sun. Do NOT apply rubbing alcohol (strips silk dyes), acetone (destroys silk), peroxide (yellows silk), OxiClean, or any cleaner. Silk's best outcome is reaching the pro with nothing applied.

CALL FIRST — "Do you treat lipstick on silk with cold POG + sodium hydrosulfite?" If they don't know what those are, try another cleaner. Tell them: "Lipstick on silk, [color], age [hours]. Plastic-card lift + cold-water back-flush ONLY at home. No alcohol, no peroxide, no detergent applied."

steps: 2. Plastic card FLAT-EDGE lift of waxy bulk. No smearing.
3. Cold water back-flush from the reverse, into a catch towel.
4. Press dry between white towels — no other chemistry.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop after the cold-water back-flush. Anything else home-applied risks worse damage than the lipstick.
warnings: NEVER alcohol on silk — strips silk dyes
NEVER acetone on silk — destroys fibroin
NEVER hydrogen peroxide on silk — yellows dyes
NEVER rub or smear lipstick — drives pigment deeper
Pour through, not on top — water rings

fiberSensitivities: Silk fibroin damaged by acetone + most aggressive solvents
Silk dyes stripped by isopropyl alcohol
Silk rings from water on top

materialWarnings: Pro within hours — no safe home solvent
Cotton lipstick-removal toolkit (alcohol) STRIPS silk dyes
Lift bulk only at home — no chemistry

escalation.when: Always — silk + lipstick is pro-lane within hours.
escalation.whatToTell: "Lipstick on silk, [color], age [hours]. Plastic-card lift + cold-water back-flush ONLY at home. No alcohol, no acetone, no peroxide, no detergent applied."
escalation.specialistType: professional dry cleaner specializing in silk (call ahead — confirm cold POG + sodium hydrosulfite spotting)

contextQuestions: Lipstick type — sheer / cream / matte / long-wear?
Color — light pink / red / dark / nude?
Has anyone applied alcohol / acetone / Dawn already?
Type of silk — charmeuse / chiffon / habotai?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Long-wear / matte lipsticks have more polymer + pigment — harder. Dark colors leave more dye ghost. Pre-applied alcohol = silk dye likely already stripped where applied. Pre-applied acetone = silk fibroin damaged.
